# @zakkster/lite-headless/picture

Headless wrapper around `<picture>` / `<img>` that adds reactive
load-state tracking, IntersectionObserver-based lazy load,
container-query-driven source selection, and error retry.

The HTML stays declarative -- `<picture>` + `<source>` + `<img>`.
Native `loading="lazy"` is set as a safety net; the primitive's
IntersectionObserver controls actual src assignment so consumers
get a clean `idle` вҶ’ `loading` вҶ’ `loaded` sequence for CSS
transitions (placeholder/skeleton swap, blur-up crossfade).

## Quick example

```js
import { createPicture } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/picture";

const pic = createPicture({
    src: "hero.jpg",
    placeholder: "hero-lqip.jpg",     // low-quality image placeholder
    lazy: true,
    aspectRatio: "16/9",
    onStateChange: (state) => console.log(state),
});

pic.attachRoot(pictureEl);     // data-img-state painted here
pic.attachImg(imgEl);          // primitive controls src
```

```html
<picture>
    <source type="image/avif" srcset="hero.avif">
    <source type="image/webp" srcset="hero.webp">
    <img alt="">       <!-- src set by primitive -->
</picture>
```

## Options

| Option              | Default     | Notes                                          |
| ------------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `src`               | (required)  | Main image src                                 |
| `sources`           | `[]`        | Reserved for future use                        |
| `placeholder`       | -           | LQIP src shown until main loads                |
| `lazy`              | `true`      | Defer load via IntersectionObserver            |
| `eager`             | `false`     | Override lazy; load immediately                |
| `aspectRatio`       | -           | Sets data-aspect-ratio (CSS can use this)      |
| `containerSources`  | -           | `[{minWidth, maxWidth, src}, ...]` for ResizeObserver-driven selection |
| `maxRetries`        | `2`         | Exponential backoff between retries            |
| `rootMargin`        | `"200px"`   | IntersectionObserver margin                    |
| `onStateChange`     | -           | `(state) => void`                              |
| `onLoad` / `onError`| -           | callbacks                                      |

## State machine

```
   construct
       в”Ӯ
       в–ј
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в”Ӯidle в”Ӯ  lazy mode, not yet in viewport
   в””в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”ҳ
      в”Ӯ intersects (or eager mode)
      в–ј
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в”Ӯ loading  в”Ӯ  src assigned, awaiting load event
   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
         в”Ӯ
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
 onLoad          onError
   в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
   в–ј                в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    retry < max в”ҖвҶ’ back to loading (with backoff)
в”Ӯloadedв”Ӯ    retry exhaust в”ҖвҶ’ error
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

The state is mirrored to `data-img-state` on both the root element
and the `<img>` element so CSS can transition between placeholder
and full image:

```css
[data-img-state="idle"] [data-pic-img] {
    opacity: 0;
}
[data-img-state="loading"] [data-pic-img] {
    opacity: 0.4;
    filter: blur(8px);
}
[data-img-state="loaded"] [data-pic-img] {
    opacity: 1;
    filter: none;
    transition: opacity 200ms, filter 200ms;
}
[data-img-state="error"] [data-pic-img] {
    display: none;
}
[data-img-state="error"] [data-pic-error] {
    display: flex;
}
```

## containerSources вҖ” element-driven art direction

Native `<picture>` selects sources based on the viewport (via
`media="..."` on `<source>` tags). When you want sources selected
based on the actual element's container width (a thumbnail in a
grid is a different ideal size than the same image in a hero),
pass a `containerSources` array. The primitive uses a
ResizeObserver to re-evaluate when the element resizes.

```js
createPicture({
    src: "default.jpg",
    containerSources: [
        { maxWidth: 320, src: "thumb.jpg" },
        { minWidth: 321, maxWidth: 800, src: "medium.jpg" },
        { minWidth: 801, src: "large.jpg" },
    ],
});
```

The picker chooses the most specific match (smallest matching range).
Falls back to `src` if no match.

## Aspect ratio

Set `aspectRatio: "16/9"` (or any CSS aspect-ratio value) to add
`data-aspect-ratio="16/9"` to both root and img. Consumer CSS:

```css
[data-pic-root] img {
    aspect-ratio: var(--ar, attr(data-aspect-ratio));
    /* OR with fallback:
    aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; */
    width: 100%;
    height: auto;
}
```

Setting aspect-ratio prevents layout shift when the image loads.

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-picture src="hero.jpg" aspect-ratio="16/9" placeholder="lqip.jpg">
    <picture data-pic-root>
        <source type="image/avif" srcset="hero.avif">
        <source type="image/webp" srcset="hero.webp">
        <img data-pic-img alt="Hero">
    </picture>
</lite-picture>
```

### Attributes

| Attribute      | Effect                                  |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `src`          | required; the main image src            |
| `aspect-ratio` | `"16/9"` etc                            |
| `lazy`         | flag (default behavior)                 |
| `eager`        | flag (overrides lazy)                   |
| `placeholder`  | LQIP src                                |
| `max-retries`  | default 2                               |

### Imperative API + events

```js
host.retry()
host.state    // current state

// events
host.addEventListener("statechange", e => e.detail.state);
host.addEventListener("load",        e => {});
host.addEventListener("error",       e => e.detail.error);
```

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
